citations.description: show_ticket('59b7-69b6-e763-4a83'): ticket status is 'in_progress'; the only comment body is the E1 DESCOPE NOTE from 2026-07-16, which contains no Gerrit change number, merge commit, or vote records.

findings.criterion: [operator-attested] Landed through Gerrit: change carries `LLM-Review +1` AND `Verified +1` and is submitted to main; `make lint && make typecheck && make test` are green on the change. Evidence recorded on this ticket (change number, merge commit, vote outcomes).
findings.detail: No concrete attestation is recorded on the ticket. The only comment is the E1 DESCOPE NOTE (2026-07-16) with no Gerrit change number, merge commit SHA, or vote outcomes. The ticket status is 'in_progress', not submitted/closed. The operator-attested criterion requires ≥1 verifiable specific (a reference id/URL, named vote outcome, merge commit, or timestamp) recorded on the ticket, and none is present.
findings.remediation: Record a ticket comment (or artifact) naming the Gerrit change number (e.g. Change-Id or URL), the observed vote outcomes (LLM-Review +1, Verified +1), and the merge commit SHA + date. That concrete attestation is what makes this operator-attested criterion pass.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
